* Vendored SDK-less macOS cross-compilation support (issue #25 phase 2)

Contents:

- include/ :: the darwin libc headers as curated by the Zig project
  (ziglang/zig, lib/libc/include/any-darwin-any/, commit 738d2be9d6b6).
- libSystem.tbd :: Zig's TAPI linker stub for /usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib
  (ziglang/zig, lib/libc/darwin/libSystem.tbd, same commit). Covers
  x86_64-macos and arm64-macos (plus catalyst/arm64e variants).

How it is used: targets.til (macos_cross_args) adds

  -fuse-ld=lld -nostdlibinc -isystem vendor/macos/include -Lvendor/macos

to macos-target builds when the HOST is not a mac, so the host's stock
clang compiles against these headers and lld links against the
libSystem stub (lld ad-hoc code-signs arm64 Mach-O, which Apple
Silicon requires). The real libSystem.dylib binds at runtime on the
target machine -- its ABI is stable, same idea as mingw import libs.
Native mac builds do NOT use this directory: they get the real SDK.
Apple frameworks (Cocoa/OpenGL/...) are NOT vendored -- mode gui
cross-builds stay mac-native until phase 3 (see issues/open/25.org).

Licensing: Zig's own code is MIT. The headers originate in Apple's
SDKs / open source releases and carry their original per-file notices
(APSL and BSD-style); Zig redistributes them in-tree and til follows
the same practice. Review the per-file notices before making further
redistribution decisions.

Upgrade: re-copy the two paths above from a newer ziglang/zig checkout
(sparse clone works: git clone --depth 1 --filter=blob:none --sparse,
then git sparse-checkout set lib/libc/include/any-darwin-any
lib/libc/darwin).
